mnemonics.title: WAVE Planning Strategy
mnemonics.content: WAVE: Wave analysis → Align capacity → Validate patterns → Execute adjustments

mnemonics.title: Demand Response Levels
mnemonics.content: PROBE: Proactive forecasting → Reactive buffering → Off-peak scheduling → Balanced load → Elastic scaling

acronyms.usage: Metric to measure demand variability; higher PAR indicates more aggressive capacity planning needs
Cloud feature to automatically provision/release resources based on real-time demand
Contract defining expected service standards and performance targets

visualTips.visualization: Picture a ocean wave with horizontal line (capacity). Above line = peak demand (surge), below = trough (low demand). Wave crest = maximum load, trough = minimum usage.
Think of a spare tire in car - reserved but available when needed. Draw as dotted outline around main capacity block.
See multiple servers as lanes on highway. Traffic (requests) distributed evenly across all lanes to prevent congestion.

analogies.analogy: Like a surfer catching a wave - you don't fight the wave, you ride it. In IT, you don't fight demand fluctuations, you plan for them and work with the natural patterns.
Like a restaurant keeping extra tables reserved for walk-ins during busy hours - they're not always used but prevent turning away customers.
Like a rubber band - stretches (adds capacity) when pulled (demand increases), shrinks (releases resources) when released (demand decreases).
